title: What to Expect When Hiring a General Contractor in Westchester County, NY
excerpt: Thinking about a home renovation or addition in Westchester County? Here's what you need to know before hiring a general contractor — from licensing requirements to what questions to ask.
content:
Hiring a general contractor in Westchester County, NY is one of the most important decisions you'll make for your home. Whether you're planning a full-scale renovation, a home addition, or a kitchen remodel, the right contractor can mean the difference between a project that adds lasting value and one that causes stress and cost overruns.

In New York State, general contractors are required to be licensed and insured. In Westchester County specifically, contractors must hold a valid Westchester County Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) license. Before signing any contract, ask to see proof of both their license and their general liability insurance certificate. A reputable Westchester contractor will have these documents readily available.

Questions to Ask Before Hiring

When interviewing contractors in Westchester, ask the following: How long have you been working in Westchester County? Can you provide local references from similar projects? Are you a licensed architect as well as a contractor? The last question matters more than most homeowners realize — firms that provide both architecture and construction services under one roof can eliminate the coordination gap between designer and builder, keeping your project on track from start to finish.

What to Look for in Bids

When comparing bids from Westchester contractors, the lowest number is rarely the right choice. A thorough bid reflects a thorough understanding of your project — including structural considerations, permitting requirements, and the quality of materials specified. Be wary of vague scopes of work or bids that lack detail. A contractor who takes the time to understand your home before pricing the job is one who will manage it with the same care during construction.

Why Architecture and Construction Under One Roof Matters

Many Westchester homeowners begin a renovation by hiring an architect separately from a contractor, only to discover that the two parties have different interpretations of the drawings — leading to change orders, delays, and unexpected complications. A firm like House Architecture & Construction, PLLC eliminates this problem entirely. Our team manages both the architectural design and the physical construction, ensuring that the vision conceived on paper is executed exactly as intended in the field.
